description_long: This barn features a traditional timber-framed structure with a prominent gable roof and a secondary, slightly elevated gable section topped with a chimney. The exterior walls are clad in a pattern of light-colored panels bordered by dark wooden beams, evoking a half-timbered architectural style. The front facade includes two large arched doorways at ground level, separated by a central passage, and several small, square-paned windows symmetrically arranged above. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark shingles, providing a classic rural silhouette. The barn's overall form is elongated, with the main section extending horizontally and a taller, narrower section rising above one end. The structure appears robust and functional, suitable for agricultural storage or livestock shelter in an urban or peri-urban setting.
description: A timber-framed barn with a steep gable roof, arched double doorways, and symmetrical small windows, featuring a half-timbered exterior and a prominent chimney.
description_view_0: The barn is seen from the narrow gable end, highlighting the steep roof pitch, chimney, and timber-framed facade.
description_view_1: The front view shows two large arched doorways, a central passage, and symmetrically placed windows above, with the taller section and chimney on the right.
description_view_2: The rear gable end is visible, showing the chimney and the continuation of the timber-framed pattern without doors or windows.
description_view_3: The side view reveals the elongated structure, the elevated gable section with chimney, and the absence of doors, with windows only on the taller section.
attribute_roof_type: dual gable roof with a main steeply pitched section and a secondary, elevated gable extension
attribute_siding_pattern: horizontal half-timbered pattern with infill panels between dark wooden beams
attribute_door_style: two large arched hinged doors at the front, separated by a central passage
attribute_window_arrangement: four small, square-paned windows symmetrically placed above the doors and on the taller section
